title: IRS Code 898 Meaning (Refund Offset to Non-IRS Debt) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 898 means a refund was offset to non-IRS debt (Treasury Offset Program). See how it ties to codes 846, 570, and 971, and document it in a client-ready report.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 898 Meaning

IRS Code 898 usually means a refund offset to non-IRS debt through the Treasury Offset Program (TOP). This page explains what it signals, how to reconcile the math with the net refund, and what to tell the client (without guessing).

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 898?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 898 generally indicates the IRS (through TOP) applied some or all of the refund to a non-IRS debt. It’s an “offset” entry, not a “refund issued” entry.

Client-friendly summary
"Part of your refund was applied to another debt"
Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Typical context
Refund reduced by offset, remaining refund may still issue
Usually appears near
Codes 846, 570, 971

When does Code 898 appear?

  • After a refund is in play: The return is processed and a refund amount exists to be reduced.
  • After an offset match: TOP matches the taxpayer to a qualifying non-IRS debt and applies the offset.
  • After the IRS nets the outcome: The transcript reflects the offset and then shows the net result (which may still include a refund issuance).

What does Code 898 usually signal?

If this appears with Code 846

This is the common “net refund” story: Code 898 reduces the refund, and Code 846 may still issue the remainder. The simplest check is whether refund expected − 898 offset = refund issued (or close, after other adjustments).

If this appears with Code 570 or Code 971

A hold or notice can coexist with an offset. Don’t assume one caused the other. Use dates and the module balance to explain the sequence and what’s still pending.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Amount: Record the offset amount (Code 898) and treat it as a reduction to the refund.
  2. Math: Reconcile expected refund vs offset vs any refund issuance (often Code 846).
  3. Message: Tell the client the offset is handled via TOP, and details/disputes typically go through the offset program, not your transcript parser.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 898 can appear in a client-ready transcript report with context:

Refund Offset Timeline (Simplified)
Code 898 – Refund offset to non-IRS debt (TOP)
Amount: $1,200 • Date: 03/01/2024
Code 846 – Refund issued (net)
Amount: $800 • Date: 03/04/2024
Code 971 – Notice issued (often informational)
Note: Use dates to narrate what happened first

FAQ

What does IRS Code 898 mean on a tax transcript?

IRS Code 898 generally means a refund was offset to a non-IRS debt through TOP. The amount shown reflects what was applied away from the refund.

Can a refund still be issued if Code 898 appears?

Yes. Code 898 can reduce the refund, but a remaining amount may still be issued. Look for the refund issuance transaction (often Code 846) and compare amounts.

Does IRS Code 898 mean the IRS took the refund for taxes owed?

Not necessarily. Code 898 typically relates to non-IRS debt offsets via TOP. Always reconcile the net refund outcome on the transcript.
